raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation asks to strengthen a research passage whose thesis, gap, and contribution framing are central to perceived force.","R1.2":"Making the passage stronger centrally involves checking whether the concern, method, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The artifact contains empirical claims about coefficients and ranking changes, so strengthening requires aligning claims with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"The passage is a draft-style research narrative, so section-level ordering and argumentative arc are central to making it stronger.","R1.6":"The wording, register, concision, and paragraph flow are central because the delegation is phrased as an improvement request on prose.","RX.1":"Any projection must disclose uncertainty because the delegation is broad and does not specify a single strengthening mode.","RX.2":"The projection must avoid assigning responsibilities such as full novelty validation or citation audit beyond what the request clearly implies.","RX.3":"The task is only to project responsibility and not to revise, critique, or execute changes on the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should avoid encouraging stronger claims in ways that could misrepresent evidence or inflate research conclusions.","RX.5":"The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks to strengthen a research passage whose thesis, gap, and contribution framing are central to perceived force.
rationale.R1.2: Making the passage stronger centrally involves checking whether the concern, method, results, and conclusion form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact contains empirical claims about coefficients and ranking changes, so strengthening requires aligning claims with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The passage is a draft-style research narrative, so section-level ordering and argumentative arc are central to making it stronger.
rationale.R1.6: The wording, register, concision, and paragraph flow are central because the delegation is phrased as an improvement request on prose.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must disclose uncertainty because the delegation is broad and does not specify a single strengthening mode.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must avoid assigning responsibilities such as full novelty validation or citation audit beyond what the request clearly implies.
rationale.RX.3: The task is only to project responsibility and not to revise, critique, or execute changes on the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should avoid encouraging stronger claims in ways that could misrepresent evidence or inflate research conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The responsibility assignment should remain traceable to specific features of the delegation and artifact.
